2606.24965.title: Project Auto-World: Towards Automated Benchmarking of Neural Relational Reasoners
2606.24965.paper_summary: Reasoning about relational structures remains a significant challenge for neural models, particularly when they must systematically apply learned knowledge to problem instances that are harder than those seen in training. Progress is hampered by the difficulty of evaluating such generalization, since a priori, it is rarely clear what makes an instance hard. We study how this issue can be addressed by using large language models (LLMs) to automate benchmark generation, learning to produce increasingly challenging instances in an end-to-end manner. Concretely, given a world parametrized by Datalog rules, and an Edge Transformer as the reasoning evaluator, we use LLM-driven evolutionary search (based on FunSearch) and autonomous agentic search to discover sampling functions that yield hard problem instances. We also show that the Edge Transformer can be improved using this data such that it generalizes well to further data perturbations. Finally, we show that the same machinery can be applied to novel worlds proposed by LLMs, opening the door to autonomous research on neural relational reasoning.
2606.24965.paper_summary_zh: 推理關於關聯結構對於神經模型來說仍然是一個重大的挑戰，特別是當它們必須系統性地將學到的知識應用於比訓練中看到的更難的問題實例時。進展受到評估這種泛化的困難所阻礙，因為事先很少清楚什麼使一個實例變得困難。我們研究如何通過使用大型語言模型（LLMs）來自動生成基準，學習以端到端的方式生成越來越具挑戰性的實例來解決這個問題。具體而言，給定由Datalog規則參數化的世界，以及作為推理評估器的邊緣Transformer，我們使用基於FunSearch的LLM驅動進化搜索和自主代理搜索來發現產生困難問題實例的抽樣函數。我們還表明，邊緣Transformer可以利用這些數據進行改進，以便能夠很好地泛化到進一步的數據擾動。最後，我們展示了相同的機制可以應用於LLMs提出的新世界，為神經關聯推理的自主研究打開了大門。
2606.24965.author: Anirban Das et.al.
2606.24965.authors: Anirban Das, Joanne Boisson, Irtaza Khalid, Sumita Garai, Steven Schockaert
